channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he was separated from a family hours before they froze to death
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian national who survived a treacherous trek across the U.S.-Canada border in blizzard conditions testified Wednesday that he got separated from a family of four shortly before they froze to death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden has become notably quiet after the 2024 election and Democrats' loss
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has been notably quiet since the Democrats' gut-wrenching defeat at the polls.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Meta's Threads app launches custom feeds amid Bluesky surge
item.description: The rollout came just days after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced last week that Threads was testing the new feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: FEMA head sounds the alarm over disaster funding after double hurricanes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The money available to help communities hit by disasters has shrunk after back-to-back hurricanes Helene and Milton, and the funding problems might jeopardize the government's ability to respond to new emergencies in the future, the Federal Emergency Management Agency chief said We
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court ruling stops Louisiana from requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms for now
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Louisiana's plan to make all of the state's public school classrooms post the Ten Commandments next year remains on hold under an order Wednesday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans. The 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Trump has named his former acting attorney general, Matthew Whitaker, as the next ambassador to NATO, selecting him to fill the role at a crucial time amid Russia's war in Ukraine and tensions over defense spending across the Western security alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas man facing eviction tried to kill puppies, police say
item.description: A man allegedly tried to kill several puppies by covering them in cleaning products and placing them in a trash bag in a dumpster because he was getting evicted, according to police documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia election workers seek more penalties against Giuliani, saying he continues to defame them
item.description: Two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani have asked a judge to penalize him even more.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amid X exodus, Bluesky CEO says 1M people per day joined in past week
item.description: The social media platform Bluesky has emerged as a popular alternative for users departing Elon Musk's X and other platforms, with nearly 1 million people joining each day over the past week, Bluesky CEO Jay Graber said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “ radicals, zealots and Marxists.” He has picked Linda McMahon, a former wrestling executive, to lead the department.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mattel releases doll honoring EGOT winner Rita Moreno
item.description: Toy maker Mattel is releasing a doll honoring Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony (EGOT) winner Rita Moreno, a prominent Latina actor.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Minimum wage isn't going up: California votes against Prop 32
item.description: Californians are projected to vote against a minimum wage boost in the state, giving a "no" to Proposition 32, according to an Associated Press race call on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-Maryland teacher to serve fraction of 30-year sentence after pleading guilty to sex with teen student
item.description: A former Maryland middle school teacher was sentenced to prison on Friday after pleading guilty to having sex with a 14-year-old student dozens of times in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Almost 100 House Democrats push for Gaetz Ethics report’s release
item.description: Nearly 100 House Democrats wrote to the House Ethics Committee, urging them to publish its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and their investigation into his alleged misconduct after President-elect Trump nominated him to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Prosecutors finished calling witnesses Tuesday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, and the defense began presenting its case but quickly ran into snags. Jose Ibarra, 26, is charged with murder and other crimes in Riley’s February death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta to begin serving Shake Shack on select flights
item.description: Travelers will soon be able to enjoy Shake Shack's famous burgers on select Delta flights beginning next month, with plans to expand throughout the U.S. in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: These are the best cruise lines for 2025
item.description: If you're looking to spend a vacation at sea but haven't decided which cruise line is best for your specific needs, a new online ranking shows the best options for every type of traveler, from families to luxury seekers. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Donald Trump to nominate Dr. Oz for Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump plans to nominate former Pennsylvania Senate nominee Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Administrator.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: FEMA administrator says she supports investigation of alleged Trump bias in relief efforts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency told lawmakers Tuesday she has encouraged the agency's inspector general to review whether an employee was acting alone when directing workers helping hurricane victims not to go to homes with yards signs supporting President-elec
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump chooses TV doctor Mehmet Oz to lead Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump says he is nominating Dr. Mehmet Oz, who hosted a long-running television talk show, to lead the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Another rare 'doomsday fish' discovered off Southern California coast for the third time this year
item.description: Another oarfish has made its way from the deep sea to the coast of San Diego County in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KTLA 5

item.title: Rare coin found in piece of furniture sells for $2.52M at auction
item.description: A rare 17th Century coin sold at auction for a record-breaking $2.52 million eight years after it was found in an old cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates the market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former 'American Idol' contestant pleads guilty in deadly DUI crash
item.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Kennedy has pleaded guilty to driving under the influence during a deadly 2022 crash in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Robert Cox, KRON4

item.title: John Stamos wears bald cap to show support for Dave Coulier amid cancer diagnosis
item.description: John Stamos is showing his support for his former "Full House" co-star, Dave Coulier, amid his cancer diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Mountain lion rescued from tree outside California home
item.description: First responders rescued a young mountain lion from a tree after he wandered into the yard of an Orange County home on Monday afternoon
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: 3 killed in series of random stabbings in New York City
item.description: A man is accused of fatally stabbing three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Mom's donated kidney to Oregon son turns 100
item.description: Ken Reese remembers how selfless his mother, Doris, was. When he was a high school student 49 years ago, his mom gave him a new lease on life by donating one of her kidneys to him.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dinh, KRON4

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Federal appeals court won't block upcoming nitrogen gas execution in Alabama
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — A federal appeals court said Monday it will let the nation’s third execution with nitrogen gas go forward this week in Alabama, rejecting arguments that the new method causes unconstitutional levels of pain. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plastic bag bans can sometimes backfire: Study
item.description: Policies aimed at curbing the use of single-use plastic bags have sometimes accomplished the opposite of their intentions, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, WGN 9

item.title: Advocates urge Biden to dismantle migrant detention before Trump takes office
item.description: Nearly 200 migrant advocacy groups have asked President Joe Biden to dismantle U.S. immigration detention facilities before President-Elect Donald Trump takes office, and to give humanitarian parole to some migrants currently detained.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Steve Bannon's trial in a border wall charity scheme case delayed until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon 's border wall fundraising trial was postponed to February as prosecutors disclosed Monday that the former Trump White House strategist himself once suggested the nonprofit venture was “a scam.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man attacked by owls twice in less than a week
item.description: Owls aren't notoriously menacing creatures, but those living in the Old Shell Road area might not agree.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WFLA, NBC

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: About one in five Americans – and a virtually identical share of Republicans and Democrats – regularly get their news from digital influencers who are more likely to be found on the social media platform X, according to a report released Monday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to stop using the same password over and over
item.description: The most common password is 123456. How long does it take to crack that? Less than one second. That's the findings from NordPass, which has been studying the most common passwords for six years. It's time to start using a password manager. Here's how.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5
